mission.title: dapr: Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr
mission.description: Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
## Overview

Hi guys. Maybe Dapr could provide an outbox & inbox infrastructure since any distributed app development framework needs it to make sure domain events are published and subscribed.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr
steps.description:
Outbox doesn't need time based scheduling, but where this might make a lot of sense is for delayed pubsub scheduling. The only issue I see at the moment for using actor reminders to schedule pub/sub activities is the RPS limitation that is imposed based on the current design. This leads back to our
```yaml
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: statestore
spec:
  type: state.mongodb
  version: v1
  - name: outboxPublishPubsub # Required
    value: mypubsub
  - name: outboxPublishTopic # Required
    value: orders
  - name: outboxPubsub # Optional
    value: myoutboxpubsub
  - name: outboxStateScanDelay # Optional
    value: 5s
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Outbox pattern for statestore + pubsub in Dapr" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Outbox doesn't need time based scheduling, but where this might make a lot of sense is for delayed pubsub scheduling. The only issue I see at the moment for using actor reminders to schedule pub/sub activities is the RPS limitation that is imposed based on the current design. This leads back to our discussion on improving the actor reminder system.

apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: statestore
spec:
  type: state.mongodb
  version: v1
  - name: outboxPublishPubsub # Required
    value: mypubsub
  - name: outboxPublishTopic # Required
    value: orders
  - name: outboxPubsub # Optional
    value: myoutboxpubsub
  - name: outboxStateScanDelay # Optional
    value: 5s

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
